gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (67/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (67/ 9)

parts.text: جَدِّهِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ

text: كَانَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ إِذَا غَزَوْا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ مَرُّوا بِمَكَانٍ كَثِيرِ الشَّجَرِ ثُمَّ خَرَجُوا إِلَى الْفَضَاءِ نَظَرَ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَى بَعْضٍ فَتَصَافَحُوا .

en.hubeali: From him, from Musa Bin Al Qasim, from his grandfather Muawiya Bin Wahab, or someone else, from Razyn,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It was so that whenever the Muslims used to go on a military expedition with Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and pass by a place with a lot of trees, then go out to the open sky, some of them used to look at the others and shake their hands’.
en.sarwar: 12. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Musa ibn al-Qasim from his grandfather, Mu’awiyah ibn Wahab or from others from Razin from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “During an armed expedition the Muslims, along with the Messenger of Allah, each time after passing through a wooded area on reaching the open would look at each other and shake hands.”
